title: selinux issue when writing in container with 'buildah run'
body:
**Description**
I have come across multiple (what appear to be) SELinux issues when running `buildah run` which I am trying to narrow down. I initially had SELinux permission denied errors on an OL7 system, and have since built a clean CentOS 7 VM in an attempt to create a reproducer to post here. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. I have created 'clean' ext4 mount for buildah specific directories to reproduce/isolate the issue, this includes creating the following directories:
`/var/lib/buildah/storage`
`/var/run/buildah/storage` (tmpfs /run/buildah/storage)

The permissions on these directories were configured to match the existing `containers` directories using `chcon --reference`
  
Specifically:  
`unconfined_u:object_r:container_var_lib_t:s0`
`unconfined_u:object_r:container_var_run_t:s0`

The buildah configuration was updated in:
`/etc/containers/storage.conf`

With the following configuration:
`runroot = /var/run/buildah/storage`
`graphroot = /var/run/buildah/storage`

2. Build an image from `scratch`

3. Attempt to run any command in the container with SELinux enabled / enforcing
`# buildah run working-container -- touch /tmp/myfile`
`touch: cannot touch '/tmp/myfile': Permission denied`
`# setenforce 0`
`# buildah run working-container -- touch /tmp/myfile`
`(works successfully)`

**Describe the results you received:**
Error writing to filesystem, AVC denial.
`type=AVC msg=audit(1522760070.369:257): avc:  denied  { write } for  pid=3851 comm="touch" name="tmp" dev="dm-5" ino=132685 scontext=system_u:system_r:svirt_lxc_net_t:s0:c263,c706 tcontext=system_u:object_r:tmp_t:s0 tclass=dir`

**Describe the results you expected:**
Successful execution of command without disabling SELinux

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-0.12-1.git8badcc2.el7.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:       0.12
Go Version:    go1.8.3
Image Spec:    1.0.0
Runtime Spec:  1.0.0
Git Commit:    8badcc2
Built:         Tue Feb 13 07:35:11 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
CentOS Linux release 7.4.1708 (Core)
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
3.10.0-693.21.1.el7.x86_64 #1 SMP Wed Mar 7 19:03:37 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `rpm -q selinux-policy`:**

```
selinux-policy-3.13.1-166.el7_4.9.noarch
```

**Output of `rpm -q runc`:**

```
runc-1.0.0-27.rc5.dev.git4fc53a8.el7.centos.x86_64 (built from upstream, rc4 shows same behaviour)
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
 storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
#runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"
runroot = "/var/run/buildah/storage"

# Primary Read/Write location of container storage
#graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/buildah/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional Read/Only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers.
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"
```

comments.body:
Try 
semanage fcontext -a -e /var/lib/containers /var/lib/buildah
restorecon -R -v /var/lib/buildah

You changed the default labeling layout,  These commands should fix your issues.  Perhaps we should modify the man page in storage.conf to explain these commands.

Thanks for the response @rhatdan 

I didn't expect that the file context would need to be defined as buildah creates all contents below the `storage` levels. Understand if this is required for the new file creation and will keep it in mind in future.

That being said, the fcontext addition and resotorecon (although making modifications) didn't resolve the issue, but it has changed slightly:

```
# buildah run working-container -- touch /tmp/randomfile
touch: cannot touch '/tmp/randomfile': Permission denied
```

The AVC denial now looks like this:
`type=AVC msg=audit(1522763209.848:319): avc:  denied  { write } for  pid=4596 comm="touch" name="tmp" dev="dm-5" ino=132685 scontext=system_u:system_r:svirt_lxc_net_t:s0:c263,c706 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_share_t:s0 tclass=dir`

Is this a Centos or RHEL system?  What version of the OS and What version of container-selinux

This looks weird in that the write should be happening on the upper layer with overlay.

Looks like it is 7.4. 
rpm -q container-selinux

CentOS 7.4
`container-selinux-2.42-1.gitad8f0f7.el7.noarch`

I see the virt7-container-common-candidate repo has a newer container-selinux
`container-selinux-2.54-1.el7.noarch`

-edit-
Found the release. Should I bump this up?

-edit 2-
Upgraded it, same AVC denial result.
If you run buildah without changing the default location does it work fine with SELinux?
@TomSweeneyRedHat Tom do you have a handy centos box that you could try this with buildah to see if you get the same problems?
@rh-atomic-bot I don't, I was fighting space issues and burned the vm and the kit .
Ok maybe try it on Fedora and see if you have issues there?
Will do

Why not mount the new disk on /var/lib/containers/ then?  BTW I don't think the placement is the issue. I am clueless what is going on now.

When you mount a container could you put in the in 
```
mount | grep overlay
```

@rhatdan, the reason I chose /var/lib/buildah is that systems I am adding buildah to are existing shared systems and `containers` was ambiguous when others are using existing docker config on the same system, I didn't want the potential confusion.

That aside, in this test VM I created a new ext4 mount, mounted it in  `/var/lib/containers` (started fresh). Updated `/etc/containers/storage.conf` and same result:

```
# buildah run working-container -- touch /tmp/randomfile
touch: cannot touch '/tmp/randomfile': Permission denied
```

AVC Denial
`type=AVC msg=audit(1522767255.369:360): avc:  denied  { write } for  pid=5971 comm="touch" name="tmp" dev="dm-3" ino=263757 scontext=system_u:system_r:svirt_lxc_net_t:s0:c178,c842 tcontext=system_u:object_r:tmp_t:s0 tclass=dir`

Mount info:
`# mount | grep overlay`
`/dev/mapper/centos-lv_var_lib_containers on /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay type ext4 (rw,relatime,seclabel,data=ordered)`

Could you do a 
buildah mount working-container
And then see what overlay says?

```
# buildah mount working-container
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/c1d22171101a5b1463f9cc99970fdf8a5afb14ba1eec6250f6c100c531edac55/diff

# buildah mount
89b7b195c8dc /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/c1d22171101a5b1463f9cc99970fdf8a5afb14ba1eec6250f6c100c531edac55/diff

# mount | grep overlay
/dev/mapper/centos-lv_var_lib_containers on /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay type ext4 (rw,relatime,seclabel,data=ordered)
```

It may be worth noting I found the following in dmesg:
```
[Wed Apr  4 08:04:32 2018] SELinux: mount invalid.  Same superblock, different security settings for (dev mqueue, type mqueue)
[Wed Apr  4 08:19:41 2018] SELinux: mount invalid.  Same superblock, different security settings for (dev mqueue, type mqueue)
```

So the overlays are not being mounted with a context flag.
This is what I see.
```
# buildah mount alpine-image-working-container
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/1441bb8c3056d82874845df18197c8fe106d11729f1f8f9f4e9b1621b9c2cab1/merged
# mount | grep overlay 
/dev/mapper/fedora-var on /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay type xfs (rw,relatime,seclabel,attr2,inode64,noquota)
overlay on /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/1441bb8c3056d82874845df18197c8fe106d11729f1f8f9f4e9b1621b9c2cab1/merged type overlay (rw,relatime,context="system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c750,c835",lowerdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/S3VNJBV6DVMAA7LPUFVAZYRCGT:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/5QIEUIPAELBJC3MKFTAS7FVG5J,upperdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/1441bb8c3056d82874845df18197c8fe106d11729f1f8f9f4e9b1621b9c2cab1/diff,workdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/1441bb8c3056d82874845df18197c8fe106d11729f1f8f9f4e9b1621b9c2cab1/work)
```

Notice the context= string above.

Did you build your own buildah or are you using the system buildah?

The buildah 0.11 is from CentOS extras
http://mirror.centos.org/centos/7/extras/x86_64/Packages/

The buildah 0.12 is from here (used to create above output):
https://cbs.centos.org/repos/virt7-container-common-candidate/x86_64/os/Packages/
@rhatdan, can you comment on the `SELinux: mount invalid` above. This message is returned in dmesg as result of the `buildah run` commands that attempt filesystem writes.

Those are issues with runc, and can be ignored.
Could you build buildah from git and see if it works.
Yep, will do.

Maybe I messed up something, but I got a different error on F27.

```
# chcon --reference /var/lib/containers/storage /var/lib/buildah/storage
# ls -alFZ !$
ls -alFZ /var/lib/buildah/storage
total 0
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root unconfined_u:object_r:container_var_lib_t:s0  6 Apr  3 19:02 ./
drwxr-xr-x. 3 root root unconfined_u:object_r:var_lib_t:s0           21 Apr  3 19:02 ../

# chcon --reference /run/buildah /var/run/buildah/storage
# ls -alFZ /var/run/buildah/storage
total 4
drwxr-xr-x. 8 root root unconfined_u:object_r:var_run_t:s0 180 Apr  3 19:23 ./
drwxr-xr-x. 3 root root unconfined_u:object_r:var_run_t:s0  60 Apr  3 19:03 ../
drwx------. 2 root root unconfined_u:object_r:var_run_t:s0  40 Apr  3 19:23 mounts/
drwx------. 4 root root unconfined_u:object_r:var_run_t:s0  80 Apr  3 19:23 overlay/
drwx------. 3 root root unconfined_u:object_r:var_run_t:s0 100 Apr  3 19:23 overlay-containers/
drwx------. 2 root root unconfined_u:object_r:var_run_t:s0  60 Apr  3 19:23 overlay-images/
drwx------. 2 root root unconfined_u:object_r:var_run_t:s0 100 Apr  3 19:23 overlay-layers/
-rw-------. 1 root root unconfined_u:object_r:var_run_t:s0  64 Apr  3 19:23 storage.lock
drwx------. 2 root root unconfined_u:object_r:var_run_t:s0  40 Apr  3 19:23 tmp/

# cat /etc/containers/storage.conf
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
# runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary Read/Write location of container storage
# graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

runroot = "/var/run/buildah/storage"
graphroot = "/var/run/buildah/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional Read/Only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers.
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"

# buildah from scratch
working-container

# buildah run working-container -- touch /tmp/myfile
/var/run/buildah/storage/overlay/dd3e2670d45f9825b6bf1814d1cca3db69d21b603e3a453e4418a740c2e10682/merged is not an absolute path or is a symlink

# buildah version
Version:       0.15
Go Version:    go1.9.4
Image Spec:    1.0.0
Runtime Spec:  1.0.0
Git Commit:    d1330a5
Built:         Tue Feb 27 08:05:39 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64

(Latest Buildah from dnf install, not a git built version)
```

Will play more in the morning.

Where is buildah 0.15 defined? This repo only shows tagged releases to 0.12. Can we get the tags/releases updated in this repo for newer versions? (happy to raise a new issue)
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/releases

Changelog also only shows to 0.5
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md

I will work off this
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/buildah.go#L24

-edit-

Raised issue #550

Have built a new package off the latest commit 5ce8009

```
# rpm -qa | grep buildah
buildah-0.15.1-1.git5ce8009.el7.x86_64
```

Small hurdle with first test, not sure what caused breakage here?
```
# buildah run working-container -- touch /tmp/myfile
Invalid format on container you must recreate the container
```

After rebuilding the container I am happy to report it has resolved the issue:
```
# buildah run working-container -- touch /tmp/myfile
# buildah run working-container -- ls /tmp
myfile
```

I can also confirm that this works cleanly when relocating graphroot/runroot which was the original intention.

Thanks to all involved.

Ok it looks like it is time to cut buildah-0.1.16
I wanted to wait for chain builds.  But...
@rhatdan, sounds good for all of us still clinging to 'archaic' EL7 😉
EL7 pays my salary, so keep clinging.
@pixdrift the Buildah v0.15 came from the Fedora Repository. I just did a "dnf  install" on a new Fedora 27 machine, I didn't have CentOS super handy.

Cheers @TomSweeneyRedHat, was just trying to track down (as an RH outsider) the pipeline that led to it ending up in the Fedora 27 repo as the v0.15 release isn't tagged in this repo and I thought the tagging may have been what triggered the build. I have since found the source was updated to 0.15 but not the release tags (raised issue #550 for this)
@pixdrift I updated that stuff yesterday.
@rhatdan All good, just keeping you honest. Thanks for the quick fix.
